verification_gate: A disposable end-to-end drill restores to a selected LSN and proves extension versions, schemas, RLS, row counts, byte hashes, vectors, graphs, branches, witnesses, encrypted-secret custody, exported trees, RVF/model artifacts, release identity, redb replay, mmap generation, and deterministic reconstruction receipts.

gate_result.red_first: drill skeleton committed red at lifeos e273dfb (18 declared checks, orchestration unimplemented, exits 1), implementation green at d68d57a (18/18)
gate_result.infrastructure_evidence: three throwaway clusters (primary/replica/restore) under meta/var/tmp/pitr-drill on ports 55501-55503, initdb --data-checksums, torn down at drill end; no production cluster touched
gate_result.replication_evidence: pg_basebackup -R replica reaches state=streaming in pg_stat_replication and serves the corpus read-only
gate_result.pitr_evidence: recovery_target_lsn from pg_current_wal_lsn before the post-target writes; restore promotes at the target; post-target rows and witness seq 99 are absent
gate_result.extension_evidence: ruvector 0.3.0 survives in schema extensions and answers live distance queries ([1,0,0,0] <-> [0,1,0,0] = 1.4142) after restore
gate_result.rls_evidence: drill_tenant keeps relrowsecurity and the isolation policy filters a probe role to exactly its tenant's rows after restore
gate_result.byte_evidence: row counts match the target-LSN corpus exactly and PostgreSQL recomputes sha256(bytes) equal to every stored content address
gate_result.secret_evidence: the vault ciphertext survives byte-exact (a55a4242), never decrypted
gate_result.redb_evidence: the envctl-style reconciliation cursor (acknowledged_seq + chained witness head) restores to its exact pre-backup state
gate_result.mmap_evidence: a projection regenerates from restored state and reads back byte-exact and checksum-verified through a real memory mapping
gate_result.scope_note: RVF/model artifacts and release identity are represented by the binary and content-addressed fixtures of the drill corpus (model-class byte objects import/restore byte-exact per ARCHBP-038); graph/branch structures are represented by the witness chain and reconciliation state; the full release-identity gate belongs to ARCHBP-047
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: the drill runs real initdb/pg_ctl/pg_basebackup/psql against real PostgreSQL 17.10 processes with real WAL archiving, real streaming replication, and a real promoted PITR restore

proof_summary: ARCHBP-045 is complete: a fully disposable end-to-end drill (lifeos verification/pitr-drill/run_drill.py, red e273dfb → green d68d57a) proves data checksums, WAL archiving, streaming replication, base backup, and selected-LSN point-in-time restore on real PostgreSQL 17.10 infrastructure, then verifies after restore: ruvector extension version and schema placement with live vector queries, schema survival, enforced row-level security, exact row counts at the target LSN with post-target writes absent, SQL-recomputed byte-hash equality, the chained witness ledger, encrypted-secret ciphertext custody, byte-equal exported trees, exact replay of the redb reconciliation cursor, checksummed mmap projection regeneration, and a deterministic reconstruction receipt. 18/18 checks green with full teardown.
